attributes.description:
This visualization displays the total count of instances where audit management tools were used by unauthorized personnel or roles, providing a critical high-level metric for **CCI-001493** (authorizing access to management of audit functionality). 

**Why this visualization works:** It provides a quick, high-level understanding of the overall unauthorized activity directed at audit functionality. A non-zero count here immediately flags a potential compliance issue, signaling a need for deeper investigation into your access controls. 

**How an analyst can use it:** Use this metric in conjunction with the trend visualization to understand if a high number of unauthorized attempts is a recent spike or a persistent issue. This visualization acts as a quick health check for the authorization controls on audit management. A continuously high count, even if not spiking, suggests a need for deeper investigation into why unauthorized users are attempting to manage audit functions. Remember to use global filters to focus on specific components (e.g., 'host.name: <your_component_name>').

query.query: (process.name:(auditctl OR wevtutil.exe OR auditpol OR journalctl OR powershell.exe OR cmd.exe OR bash OR wmic OR sc.exe OR reg.exe OR net.exe OR taskkill.exe OR schtasks.exe OR at.exe OR psexec.exe OR mimikatz.exe OR certutil.exe OR bitsadmin.exe)) AND (event.category:(process OR file OR registry OR network OR iam) OR event.type:(creation OR modification OR deletion OR execution OR policy_update OR service_control OR authentication OR authorization OR role_change OR user_provisioning OR user_deprovisioning)) AND NOT (user.group.name:"auditor-group" OR user.name:('authorized_user_1' OR 'admin_service_account' OR system OR root OR  administrator OR "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" OR LocalSystem OR NetworkService)) AND NOT (process.name:auditd AND event.outcome:success)

attributes.title: [AU-9, CCI-001493] Total Unauthorized Audit Tool Usage (converted)

attributes.description:
This visualization displays the distribution of access, modification, and deletion attempts on audit information and tools by different users. This provides a comprehensive view for the overall **AU-9** control, and specifically supports **CCI-001493** (authorized management). 

**Why this visualization works:** It quickly highlights which users are most frequently interacting with or attempting to interact with audit data and tools, allowing analysts to identify both authorized and potentially unauthorized high-activity users. This is crucial for verifying that only a defined subset of privileged users is managing audit functionality as per AU-9(4). 

**How an analyst can use it:** Look for unexpected users appearing with high counts, especially for modification or deletion attempts. Use global filters to narrow down by host or specific audit tools. Investigate any users who are not part of the 'organization-defined subset of privileged users' for audit management. This helps verify that access to audit management functionality is appropriately restricted across all relevant CCIs under AU-9. Remember to use global filters to focus on a specific component (e.g., 'host.name: <your_component_name>').

query.query: (process.name:(auditctl OR wevtutil.exe OR auditpol OR journalctl OR powershell.exe OR cmd.exe OR bash OR wmic OR sc.exe OR reg.exe OR net.exe OR taskkill.exe OR schtasks.exe OR at.exe OR psexec.exe OR mimikatz.exe OR certutil.exe OR bitsadmin.exe)) AND (event.category:(process OR file OR registry OR network OR iam) OR event.type:(creation OR modification OR deletion OR execution OR policy_update OR service_control OR authentication OR authorization OR role_change OR user_provisioning OR user_deprovisioning)) AND NOT (user.group.name:"auditor-group" OR user.name:('authorized_user_1' OR 'admin_service_account' OR system OR root OR  administrator OR "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" OR LocalSystem OR NetworkService)) AND NOT (process.name:auditd AND event.outcome:success)

attributes.title: [CCI-001493] Audit Activity by User (All CCIs)

attributes.description:
This table provides a detailed breakdown of individual events where audit management tools were used by unauthorized personnel or roles, providing granular evidence for **CCI-001493** (authorizing access to management of audit functionality). 

**Why this visualization works:** It offers granular detail on each event, including the timestamp, host, user, their group, the process involved, and the specific arguments used. This level of detail is crucial for in-depth investigation and pinpointing exact policy violations, allowing you to understand the 'who, what, and where' of potential compliance failures related to audit management access. 

**How an analyst can use it:** Sort by 'user.name' or 'user.group.name' to identify common sources of unauthorized activity. Examine 'process.name' and 'process.args' to understand which specific audit tools were used and how. The 'host.name' field helps to identify the component involved. This table is essential for understanding the context of each unauthorized event and verifying if your access controls are working as intended. Remember to apply global filters for specific components (e.g., 'host.name: <your_component_name>') to refine your view.

query.query: (process.name:(auditctl OR wevtutil.exe OR auditpol OR journalctl OR powershell.exe OR cmd.exe OR bash OR wmic OR sc.exe OR reg.exe OR net.exe OR taskkill.exe OR schtasks.exe OR at.exe OR psexec.exe OR mimikatz.exe OR certutil.exe OR bitsadmin.exe)) AND (event.category:(process OR file OR registry OR network OR iam) OR event.type:(creation OR modification OR deletion OR execution OR policy_update OR service_control OR authentication OR authorization OR role_change OR user_provisioning OR user_deprovisioning)) AND NOT (user.group.name:"auditor-group" OR user.name:('authorized_user_1' OR 'admin_service_account' OR system OR root OR  administrator OR "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" OR LocalSystem OR NetworkService)) AND NOT (process.name:auditd AND event.outcome:success)

attributes.title: [AU-9, CCI-001493] Detailed Unauthorized Audit Tool Usage Events

attributes.description:
This section provides Machine Learning (ML) powered visualizations to help assess compliance with CCI-001493, which focuses on authorizing access for the management of audit functionality. This visualization analyzes anomaly scores from an ML job that detects unusual usage of audit management tools.

To perform a component-level compliance assessment, utilize the global filters at the top of the Kibana dashboard. Apply filters such as 'influencers.host.name: <your_component_name>' to focus on a specific system. The ML job learns the normal patterns of tool usage by authorized users. Therefore, an anomaly could represent a compromised authorized account or an insider threat. Investigate any high-score anomalies, paying close attention to the user, the tool used, and the time of the event.
attributes.title: [AU-9-CCI-001493] ML - Unauthorized Audit Tool Usage Anomalies
attributes.visState: {"aggs":[],"params":{"fontSize":12,"markdown":"# [AU-9] CCI-001493: Authorized Audit Management (ML)\n\n### Monitor for anomalous usage of audit management tools.","openLinksInNewTab":false},"title":"[AU-9-CCI-001493] ML - Unauthorized Audit Tool Usage Anomalies","type":"markdown"}

attributes.description: This section provides visualizations to help you assess compliance with CCI-001493, focusing on ensuring that only authorized personnel can manage audit functionality. You'll find visualizations that highlight attempts to use audit management tools by unauthorized roles or personnel. To perform a component-level compliance assessment, utilize the global filters at the top of the Kibana dashboard. Apply filters like 'host.name: <your_component_name>' to focus on a specific system. Observe the trends and individual events in the visualizations for any anomalies or policy violations. Instances where users outside the defined 'auditor-group' (or your organization-defined authorized group) are executing audit management tools indicate a potential compliance failure. Pay close attention to the 'user.name' and 'user.group.name' fields to verify proper authorization. This visualization now displays the health status for CCI-001493 based on the anomaly scores from associated ML jobs.
attributes.title: [CCI-001493] Protection of Audit Information: Authorized Management ML

attributes.description:
This visualization displays the maximum anomaly score over time for the job monitoring the usage of audit management tools.

**Why this visualization works:**
It helps detect unusual behavior that might otherwise go unnoticed. For instance, an administrator using an audit tool at an odd hour, or from an unusual location, could be flagged by the ML job, whereas a simple rule-based alert would miss this context.

**How an analyst can use it:**
Look for spikes in the anomaly score. A high score indicates a significant deviation from normal, authorized behavior. Use the time selector to isolate these periods and cross-reference with the detailed table to see which user and tool are associated with the anomaly.
attributes.title: [AU-9-CCI-001493] ML - Audit Tool Usage Anomaly Score Trend
